title: Board Oversight of Technology Risk
translation: The board must actively oversee how the institution manages technology risks. This means technology risk should be part of the overall enterprise risk framework, and the board must ensure enough budget, people, and tools are dedicated to managing it.
evidence: Board meeting minutes discussing technology risk
Enterprise risk management framework incorporating technology risk
Board-approved technology risk budget and resource allocation
Board terms of reference covering technology risk oversight

artifacts.name: Board Technology Risk Charter
artifacts.description: Formal charter defining board responsibilities for technology risk oversight

artifacts.name: Board Meeting Minutes
artifacts.description: Minutes evidencing board discussion of technology risk matters

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Dashboard for Board
artifacts.description: Periodic dashboard report presented to the board on technology risk posture

title: Board Technology Risk Committee
translation: You need a dedicated board committee focused on technology risk. Its members must understand technology and cyber topics, and it must meet at least every quarter to review the current state of technology risk.
evidence: Board committee charter or terms of reference
Committee member profiles demonstrating relevant expertise
Quarterly meeting schedules and attendance records
Committee meeting minutes and action items

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Committee Terms of Reference
artifacts.description: Board-approved terms of reference for the technology risk committee

artifacts.name: Committee Meeting Minutes
artifacts.description: Minutes from quarterly technology risk committee meetings

artifacts.name: Member Competency Profiles
artifacts.description: CVs or competency records for committee members demonstrating technology expertise

title: Board Competency in Technology
verbatim: The board must designate a board-level committee which shall be responsible for supporting the board in providing oversight over technology-related matters. The composition of the designated committee must include at least one member with technology experience and competencies.
translation: Board members should either have personal expertise in technology and cybersecurity, or have access to advisors who do. Ongoing training and education programs should keep the board current on new threats and technology trends.
evidence: Board training records on technology and cybersecurity topics
External advisor engagement records
Board competency assessment results
Annual training plan for board members

artifacts.name: Board Training Records
artifacts.description: Records of board training sessions on technology and cybersecurity

artifacts.name: Board Competency Framework
artifacts.description: Framework defining expected technology competencies for board members

artifacts.name: Board Competency Assessment Report
artifacts.description: Annual assessment of board members' technology knowledge and skill gaps

title: Board Risk Appetite Statement
verbatim:
To promote effective discussion at the board level amidst a rapidly evolving technology and cyber risk landscape, the board must-
(a) obtain regular updates on technology risk and cyber threats;
(b) allocate sufficient time to discuss cyber risks, including the strategic, reputational and liquidity risks and impact of operational disruption to stakeholders which could arise from an extreme cyber incident. This shall be supported by input from external experts where appropriate; and
(c) participate in relevant cybersecurity awareness and training programmes.
translation: The board must formally approve a statement that sets out how much technology risk the institution is prepared to tolerate. This should include both measurable metrics and qualitative descriptions, and must be reviewed yearly or when circumstances change significantly.
evidence: Board-approved technology risk appetite statement
Risk appetite metrics and thresholds
Annual review and approval records
Evidence of risk appetite communication to management

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Appetite Statement
artifacts.description: Board-approved statement defining acceptable levels of technology risk

artifacts.name: Risk Appetite Review Records
artifacts.description: Evidence of annual review and board sign-off on risk appetite

artifacts.name: Risk Appetite Monitoring Report
artifacts.description: Periodic report on actual risk levels versus approved appetite thresholds

title: Board Reporting on Technology Risk
verbatim: The board audit committee (BAC) shall be responsible for ensuring the effectiveness of the internal technology audit function. This shall include ensuring the adequate competence of the audit staff to perform technology audits. The BAC shall review and ensure appropriate audit scope, procedures and frequency of technology audits. The BAC shall also ensure effective oversight over the prompt closure of corrective actions to address technology control gaps.
translation: Management must give the board regular, detailed reports on technology risk at least every quarter. Reports must cover KRIs, incident trends, remediation progress, and forward-looking risk assessments so the board can make informed decisions.
evidence: Quarterly technology risk reports to the board
Key risk indicator dashboards
Incident summary reports presented to board
Risk mitigation status reports
Board acknowledgement or sign-off records

artifacts.name: Quarterly Board Risk Report
artifacts.description: Comprehensive quarterly technology risk report presented to the board

artifacts.name: KRI Dashboard
artifacts.description: Key risk indicator dashboard with trend analysis

artifacts.name: Board Acknowledgement Records
artifacts.description: Evidence that the board received and discussed technology risk reports

title: Senior Management Accountability
verbatim:
The senior management shall bear primary responsibility for the day-to-day management of technology risks including cyber risks. In fulfilling its responsibilities, senior management must–
(a) implement board approved TRMF and CRF into specific policies and procedures that are consistent with the approved risk appetite and risk tolerance;
(b) ensure that the crisis management plan for service disruptions provides for timely escalation of recovery action, taking into consideration the impact on customers, such as:
(i) duration of disruption;
(ii) number of customers affected by a disruption; and
(iii) number and value of financial transaction impacted; and
(c) provide regular updates to the board on the status of key performance indicators with pertinent information on the risk controls to facilitate informed performance review.
translation: Senior management is directly responsible for putting the board-approved technology risk framework into practice. They must appoint a CISO (or equivalent) who can report directly to the board or its technology risk committee on cybersecurity issues.
evidence: Senior management accountability matrix for technology risk
CISO appointment letter and job description
CISO reporting line documentation
Board or committee meeting records showing CISO reporting

artifacts.name: Senior Management Accountability Matrix
artifacts.description: Document defining senior management roles and accountability for technology risk

artifacts.name: CISO Appointment and Mandate
artifacts.description: Formal appointment of CISO with defined mandate and reporting lines

artifacts.name: CISO Board Reporting Records
artifacts.description: Records of CISO presentations or reports to board or committee

title: Technology Risk Management Function
verbatim:
The senior management must establish a cross-functional committee to provide guidance on the financial institution’s technology plans and operations. The members of the committee must include senior management from both cyber and technology functions, as well as major business units. The committee’s responsibilities shall include the following:
(a) oversee the formulation and effective implementation of the strategic technology plan and associated technology policies and procedures;
(b) provide timely updates to the board on key technology matters; and
(c) approve any deviation from technology-related policies after having carefully considered a robust assessment of related risks. Material deviations shall be reported to the board.
translation: You must have a dedicated technology risk management team that operates independently from the IT operations team. This team needs proper authority, staffing, and access to systems and data to do its job of identifying, assessing, monitoring, and reporting technology risks.
evidence: Technology risk management function charter
Organisation chart showing independence from IT operations
Staffing plan and competency matrix
Function mandate and authority documentation

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Function Charter
artifacts.description: Charter defining the mandate, authority, and independence of the technology risk function

artifacts.name: Organisation Structure Chart
artifacts.description: Organisational chart demonstrating independence of risk function from IT operations

artifacts.name: Staffing and Competency Records
artifacts.description: Staffing plan and competency matrix for technology risk function personnel
